reasoning:
On December 7, 2025, Anna Muzychuk's rating on Wikipedia's FIDE rankings page is 2518. To be 1% higher, her rating must reach at least 2544 (an increase of 26 points). 

1. For Dec 14, 2025: FIDE updates its official ratings on the 1st of each month. Wikipedia mirrors this monthly update. Thus, on Dec 14, her rating on the page will definitively remain the Dec 1 value (2518). Resolves decisively NO, bounded at 0.05.

2. For Jan 6, 2026: Achieving a 26-point gain in standard Elo by the end of December requires playing many classical games and performing roughly 150-200 points above her current rating. Given her K-factor (10) and lack of major classical events scheduled for her in late December (which is traditionally dominated by the World Rapid & Blitz championships), this is highly improbable.

3. For Mar 7, 2026: She will have two additional months to play classical chess. While gaining 26 points is mechanically possible over several tournaments, top-level players very rarely experience such rapid, sustained jumps without reverting to the mean, particularly as her recent high-water mark has been in the low 2520s. We increase uncertainty slightly over time as per instructions.
